_description: Marine species encountered at Fuvahmulah dive sites. Each entry describes a species that StayWave guests can expect to see. This file can be used to dynamically render the marine life page or provide tooltips in the booking flow. To add a species, append an object with a unique id.

species.description: The signature species of Fuvahmulah. Tiger sharks are regularly encountered at cleaning stations near the channel, offering divers a front-row view of one of the ocean's most iconic predators.

en.description: The signature species of Fuvahmulah. Tiger sharks are regularly encountered at cleaning stations near the channel, offering divers a front-row view of one of the ocean's most iconic predators.

species.description: The world's largest fish passes through Fuvahmulah waters, offering unforgettable open-water encounters for lucky divers.

en.description: The world's largest fish passes through Fuvahmulah waters, offering unforgettable open-water encounters for lucky divers.

species.description: Known for their distinctive long tail fin, thresher sharks are a highlight of deep-water dives in Fuvahmulah.

en.description: Known for their distinctive long tail fin, thresher sharks are a highlight of deep-water dives in Fuvahmulah.

species.description: Great hammerhead sharks patrol the deep margins of the channel, making any sighting a memorable occasion.

en.description: Great hammerhead sharks patrol the deep margins of the channel, making any sighting a memorable occasion.

species.description: Oceanic manta rays visit cleaning stations around Fuvahmulah, gliding gracefully past divers in crystal-clear water.

en.description: Oceanic manta rays visit cleaning stations around Fuvahmulah, gliding gracefully past divers in crystal-clear water.
